questions: You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. You've been applying to jobs for a year with no offers and your savings are running out. A friend asks how the search is going. What do you say?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. Your relationship has been struggling for months despite couples therapy and difficult conversations. Your partner asks where you think things stand. What do you say?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. You've been trying to get your landlord to fix a recurring plumbing issue for six months. It's happening again. How do you respond?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. Your town's school board keeps rejecting proposals you've championed despite community support. They've done it three times now. What's your reaction?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. You've been training your dog for months and they still won't follow basic commands. Your partner asks if the training is working. What do you say?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. Your chronic pain hasn't improved despite trying every treatment your doctor suggested. They offer yet another option. How do you respond?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. You've tried to reconcile with an estranged family member multiple times and been rebuffed each time. They reach out casually. What do you feel?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. Your novel has been rejected by every publisher and agent you've submitted to over two years. Someone asks about your writing. What do you tell them?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. You've been advocating for a policy change at work for over a year and leadership keeps deprioritizing it. What's your approach now?

You're someone who never gives up and always believes that effort will eventually pay off. Your garden has failed to produce a harvest for the third consecutive season despite your best efforts. How do you feel about planting again?
